title: Running Python Code
summary: Use the ZoeSkoul browser code editor, Run button, and output panel to execute Python code.

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to Running Python Code
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In this section, you'll learn how to run Python code using the ZoeSkoul browser code editor. To get started, type your code in the editor and click the Run button. The output will appear in the output panel below. For example, you can print a message like this:

```python
print('Hello, World!')
```

Try it yourself by modifying the message!

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding Print Statements
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
The `print()` function is used to display output in Python. It can take strings, numbers, and other data types as arguments. For instance:

```python
print('This is a test message.')
```

This will show 'This is a test message.' in the output panel. Your turn: Write a print statement to display your name!

quizDraft.title: What does the print function do?
options: It saves data to a file.
It displays output on the screen.
quizDraft.hint: Think about what happens when you use print in your code.

help.concept: The print function outputs data to the console or screen.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that describes displaying information.
help.hint_2: Remember, print is used to show results, not to save or create.

quizDraft.title: Which of the following are valid print statements?
quizDraft.hint: Consider what can be passed to the print function.

help.concept: The print function can take strings and numbers as arguments.
help.hint_1: Check if the statements contain valid syntax.
help.hint_2: Remember that print can be called with or without arguments.

quizDraft.title: Print Multiple Lines
quizDraft.prompt: Write a program that prints three different messages on separate lines.
quizDraft.solutionCode:

print('Welcome to the course!')
print('Let’s start coding!')
